title: Electrodynamics and Waves — SuperBEST Cost Analysis

equations.name: Lorentz Force (3D vector)
equations.notes: General domain applies when E fields can be negative. Positive domain applies when all field components are strictly non-negative.

equations.name: EM Wave (Electric field)
equations.notes: sin counted as 1 complex EML node per SuperBEST v3 convention.

equations.name: Poynting Vector Magnitude
equations.notes: If μ₀c is precomputed as a single constant, cost reduces to 4n.

equations.name: Skin Depth

steps: div(const, ρ) or mul(1/ωμ, ρ): compound = 1n

equations.notes: With ω, μ fixed (constant medium and frequency), ωμ collapses to a single constant, reducing to 4n.

equations.name: Impedance of Free Space
equations.notes: Z₀ ≈ 376.73 Ω is a universal physical constant. In practice this can be precomputed to 0n. Listed at 4n for completeness when derived symbolically.

equations.name: Larmor Radiation Power
equations.notes: 6πε₀ treated as a single precomputed constant. Cost is the same (14n) whether 1/(6πε₀) is folded as a constant multiplier or computed inline, since c³ must still be evaluated.

equations.name: Cyclotron Frequency
equations.notes: Simplest equation in this session. Linear in both q and B.

equations.name: Phase Velocity of EM Wave
equations.notes: When μ and ε are fixed medium properties (constants), με collapses to 0n and cost reduces to 5n.

equations.name: Group Velocity

free_space_case.notes: Linear dispersion; group velocity equals phase velocity, a constant.

dispersive_media_case.notes: vg = dω/dk = ck/√(k²−k₀²). Derivative adds further ops; treat as symbolic for general analysis.

equations.notes: Reported as 0n for free-space (linear) case; 13n for dispersive ω(k) evaluation (not including differentiation overhead).

key_observations: Lorentz force dominates at 57n (general domain) due to three add(gen) calls at 11n each for adding E to cross-product components.
Cyclotron frequency is the cheapest non-trivial formula at 3n.
EM wave via complex EML sin node costs only 9n — the sin node absorbs phase computation cost efficiently.
Larmor radiation (14n) is expensive primarily due to two pow() calls on q and a plus pow(c,3).
Z₀ is a universal constant and collapses to 0n when precomputed, unlike skin depth or phase velocity which depend on variable medium properties.
Group velocity in free space is 0n (terminal constant); dispersive case requires full ω(k) evaluation.
